name: Gauss's law for the electric field
principles: Coulomb's inverse-square law in integral form
divergence theorem applied to flux of E
electric charge as the source of the electric field
conventions: E is the electric field; rho is the charge density
Q_enc is the total electric charge enclosed by the closed surface partial V
outward-pointing normal for dA on the closed surface
vacuum form: in matter, replace epsilon_0 by epsilon = epsilon_r epsilon_0 and rho by rho_free, defining D = epsilon_0 E + P with div D = rho_free
first of Maxwell's four equations; with Faraday, Ampère-Maxwell, and div B = 0, gives the full classical theory of electromagnetism
expectedLimits: Q_enc = 0: net outward flux is zero; field lines passing through the surface enter and exit in equal measure
spherically symmetric charge: E(r) = (1/(4 pi epsilon_0)) Q_enc(r) / r^2 — recovers Coulomb's law
infinite line charge (linear density lambda): E(r) = lambda / (2 pi epsilon_0 r); cylindrical Gaussian surface
infinite plane (surface density sigma): E = sigma / (2 epsilon_0) on each side; pillbox Gaussian surface
conductor in electrostatic equilibrium: E inside = 0, all excess charge on the outer surface
differential form derived from integral via divergence theorem: requires E to be sufficiently smooth (point charges produce delta functions in rho)
references: Maxwell (1865) — A Dynamical Theory of the Electromagnetic Field
BIPM — SI Brochure (defining value of epsilon_0)
